title: Roast Smoky Jamaican Yautia
description: Earthy and crunchy, this Jamaican dish features yautia, roast, and Ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly flavors that evoke a monsoon-like atmosphere.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Wrap the yautia tubers in foil and roast for 45 minutes, or until tender.
In a pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat and sauté the onion, garlic, allspice, thyme, cumin, scotch bonnet peppers, salt, and black pepper until fragrant.
Add the diced onion mixture to the roasted yautia and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and stir to coat the yautia mixture.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and roast for an additional 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Garnish with pimento berries and serve hot.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve the Roast Smoky Jamaican Yautia hot, garnished with pimento berries and a sprinkle of scotch bonnet peppers for added heat.
This dish is best served with steamed rice or roasted vegetables to soak up the flavorful sauce.
For an added crunch, sprinkle some toasted coconut flakes on top of the yautia before serving.
Adjust the seasoning as needed before serving to balance the flavors.
For a spicy kick, add more scotch bonnet peppers or use hot sauce to taste.
This recipe makes 4 servings.
Adjust the amount of scotch bonnet peppers to suit your desired level of heat and adjust the amount of coconut milk to achieve the desired consistency.
To make this dish vegan, replace the coconut milk with a plant-based alternative and omit the honey.
This recipe is suitable for diabetics and those with gluten intolerance.
This dish is best made in a cast-iron skillet or a Dutch oven for an added depth of flavor.
To make this dish in a pressure cooker, cook the yautia and onion mixture for 10 minutes, then add the coconut milk and pressure cook for an additional 5 minutes.
To make this dish in an Instant Pot, cook the yautia and onion mixture for 10 minutes, then add the coconut milk and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
This recipe makes approximately 500 calories per serving.
This recipe is high in protein and fiber, making it a nutritious and filling option for a meal or snack.
To make this recipe in advance, roast the yautia and onion mixture the day before, then assemble and roast the dish the next day.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Reheat the dish by steaming it in a steamer basket or by microwaving it on high for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
